evals.prompt: Create C4 diagrams for our internet banking system showing context and containers.
evals.expected_output: A C4 document with System Context and Container levels as Mermaid diagrams plus element catalogs.
expectations: Includes a System Context (Level 1) and a Container (Level 2) view
Uses Mermaid C4 fenced blocks (C4Context / C4Container)
Shows people/actors and system boundaries, not just technology boxes
Emits MIF frontmatter type: semantic and passes mif-validate --level 1

evals.prompt: My C4 diagram is just a box for every microservice. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Explains the abstraction levels and that Context/Container views need actors and boundaries, not a flat service map.
expectations: Identifies the missing abstraction layering (Context vs Container vs Component)
Notes that people and system boundaries are required at the Context level
Recommends starting at Level 1 and zooming in deliberately

evals.prompt: What are the four levels of the C4 model and which do I usually need?
evals.expected_output: Lists Context, Container, Component, Code and notes Code is usually omitted.
expectations: Names all four levels in order
Explains each level zooms into the previous
Notes the Code level is usually generated/omitted

evals.prompt: Upgrade this C4 model so a tool can tell whether the view is stale, what kind of document it is, and where it came from.
evals.expected_output: A Level 3 C4 document adding temporal validity, an architecture-view ontology type, W3C-PROV provenance, and a typed relationship, while keeping the Mermaid C4 diagrams.
expectations: Adds temporal (validFrom/validUntil/ttl), ontology (id architecture-view), and provenance frontmatter
Adds a typed relationships[] entry (e.g. relates-to the arc42 architecture document)
Keeps type: semantic and the Mermaid C4 diagrams intact
Passes mif-validate --level 3 with a lossless markdown<->JSON-LD round-trip
